title: Slow Cooker Beef Short Ribs
description: Fall-off-the-bone tender beef short ribs braised in a rich, savory sauce. Set it and forget it for an impressive dinner.
instructions: Season short ribs generously with salt and pepper on all sides.
Heat avocado oil in a large skillet over high heat. Sear ribs in batches until deeply browned on all sides, about 3 minutes per side. Transfer to slow cooker.
In the same skillet, sauté onion until softened, about 5 minutes. Add garlic and cook 1 minute more.
Add beef broth, coconut aminos, and tomato paste to the skillet. Stir to combine and scrape up any browned bits. Pour over ribs.
Add rosemary and thyme sprigs to the slow cooker.
Cover and cook on LOW for 8 hours or HIGH for 4 hours until meat is fall-off-the-bone tender.
Remove ribs carefully. Strain the cooking liquid and skim off excess fat. Serve ribs drizzled with sauce.

title: Lemon Herb Roasted Chicken
description: A perfectly juicy whole roasted chicken with crispy skin, infused with lemon, garlic, and fresh herbs.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Remove chicken from refrigerator 30 minutes before cooking.
Pat chicken completely dry with paper towels. Season cavity generously with salt and pepper.
Mix softened ghee with 1 tsp salt, pepper, and paprika. Gently loosen skin over breasts and thighs, and rub ghee mixture under the skin.
Stuff cavity with quartered lemon, 4 garlic cloves, half the rosemary and thyme, and quartered onion.
Rub outside of chicken with olive oil and remaining salt. Scatter remaining garlic and herbs around the chicken.
Roast for 20 minutes at 425°F, then reduce heat to 375°F (190°C) and continue roasting for 50-60 minutes until internal temperature reaches 165°F in the thickest part of the thigh.
Let rest for 15 minutes before carving. Serve with pan juices.

title: Garlic Butter Salmon
description: Succulent salmon fillets basted in garlic-infused ghee with fresh herbs. Ready in under 20 minutes.
instructions: Pat salmon fillets dry and season generously with salt and pepper on both sides.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add 2 tablespoons ghee.
Place salmon skin-side up and cook for 4 minutes until golden. Flip carefully.
Add remaining ghee and garlic to the pan. Cook for 1 minute until garlic is fragrant.
Reduce heat to medium. Continuously baste salmon with the garlic butter for 3-4 minutes until salmon is cooked through.
Remove from heat, add lemon juice and fresh herbs to the pan. Spoon sauce over salmon.
Serve immediately with lemon wedges.

title: Zucchini Noodle Bolognese
description: A hearty, slow-simmered meat sauce served over fresh zucchini noodles. All the comfort, none of the grains.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven or pot over medium heat. Add onion, carrots, and celery. Cook for 8-10 minutes until softened.
Add garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Increase heat to medium-high, add ground beef. Break up and cook until browned, about 8 minutes.
Add crushed tomatoes, tomato paste, Italian seasoning, salt, and pepper. Stir well to combine.
Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
While sauce simmers, spiralize zucchini into noodles. Just before serving, sauté zoodles in a separate pan for 2-3 minutes until slightly softened but still al dente.
Serve Bolognese sauce over zucchini noodles, garnished with fresh basil.

title: Thai Basil Chicken Stir-Fry
description: A quick and flavorful Thai-inspired stir-fry with ground chicken, fresh basil, and a savory-sweet sauce.
instructions: Mix coconut aminos, fish sauce, and lime juice in a small bowl. Set aside.
Heat avocado oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat until shimmering.
Add shallots and stir-fry for 1 minute. Add garlic and Thai chilies, cook for 30 seconds.
Add ground chicken, breaking it up into small pieces. Stir-fry for 5-6 minutes until cooked through and slightly crispy.
Add bell pepper and cook for 2 minutes.
Pour in the sauce mixture and toss to coat. Cook for 1 minute.
Remove from heat, add Thai basil leaves and toss until wilted.
Serve over cauliflower rice or on its own.

title: Grilled Steak with Chimichurri
description: Perfectly grilled ribeye steak topped with vibrant, herbaceous Argentine chimichurri sauce.
instructions: For chimichurri: Finely chop parsley, cilantro, oregano, and garlic (or pulse in food processor). Transfer to a bowl.
Add olive oil, red wine vinegar, red pepper flakes, and 0.5 tsp salt. Mix well and set aside to let flavors meld.
Remove steaks from refrigerator 30 minutes before cooking. Pat dry and season generously with remaining salt and pepper on both sides.
Preheat grill to high heat (or use a cast iron skillet over high heat).
Grill steaks for 4-5 minutes per side for medium-rare (internal temp 130°F). Adjust time for desired doneness.
Rest steaks for 10 minutes before slicing against the grain.
Serve sliced steak drizzled generously with chimichurri sauce.

title: Braised Lamb Shanks with Herbs
description: Fork-tender lamb shanks braised in red wine and aromatics. An elegant dish that's surprisingly easy to make.
instructions: Preheat oven to 325°F (165°C). Season lamb shanks generously with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown lamb shanks on all sides, about 4 minutes per side. Remove and set aside.
Add onion, carrots, and celery to the pot. Sauté for 5-6 minutes until beginning to soften.
Add garlic and cook for 1 minute. Pour in wine and scrape up any browned bits from the bottom.
Add broth, diced tomatoes, rosemary, and thyme. Return lamb shanks to the pot, nestling them into the liquid.
Bring to a simmer, then cover with lid and transfer to oven.
Braise for 2.5-3 hours until lamb is fall-off-the-bone tender.
Remove lamb shanks to a platter. Strain braising liquid and reduce if desired. Serve lamb with vegetables and sauce.

title: Mediterranean Baked Cod
description: Flaky cod baked with cherry tomatoes, olives, capers, and fresh herbs. A light yet satisfying meal.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
In a baking dish, combine cherry tomatoes, olives, capers, garlic, 2 tbsp olive oil, and half the oregano. Season with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Roast tomato mixture for 10 minutes until tomatoes begin to burst.
Season cod fillets with salt, pepper, and remaining olive oil. Nestle them into the tomato mixture.
Return to oven and bake for 12-15 minutes until cod flakes easily with a fork.
Drizzle with lemon juice and sprinkle with lemon zest, remaining oregano, and fresh basil.
Serve immediately with the tomato-olive mixture spooned over the fish.
